svg.text: SANSKRIT
PHONOLOGY & SOUND SYSTEM
SANSKRIT CONSONANT SYSTEM
5 places of articulation x 5 manner classes = 25 stops
Velars: k kh g gh ṅ
Palatals: c ch j jh ñ
Retroflexes: ṭ ṭh ḍ ḍh ṇ
Dentals: t th d dh n
Labials: p ph b bh m
Each row: voiceless plain / voiceless aspirate / voiced plain / voiced aspirate / nasal
SEMIVOWELS & SIBILANTS
y r l v (antahstha "in-between" sounds)
ś ṣ s h (three sibilants: palatal, retroflex, dental)
Three distinct /s/ sounds — preserved from Proto-Indo-European
VOWEL SYSTEM
Short: a i u r̥ l̥ / Long: ā ī ū r̥̄ (quantity distinction)
Diphthongs: e ai o au (historically ā+i, ā+u)
Syllabic /r̥/ and /l̥/: consonants that act as vowels
MAITHILI PHONOLOGY · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
MAITHILI CONSONANT SYSTEM
Inherited Sanskrit’s five-place articulation system
Retroflex stops: ṭ ṭh ḍ ḍh ṇ (preserved from Sanskrit)
Aspirates: kh gh ch jh ṭh ḍh th dh ph bh (all 10 preserved)
Maithili preserves more Sanskrit phonemes than Hindi
MAITHILI VOWELS
Seven vowels: a ā i ī u ū e o
Nasalized vowels: ã ĩ õ (Sanskrit nasal anusvara preserved)
Word-final short /a/ often silent (like Hindi, unlike Sanskrit)
PHONOLOGICAL CONSERVATION
Maithili conserves Sanskrit aspirates better than neighboring languages:
Sanskrit bhumi → Maithili bhumi "earth" (aspirate preserved)
Sanskrit karma → Maithili karma "action" (direct loan form)
Sanskrit dharma → Maithili dharma (unchanged!)
TONAL FEATURES
Some Maithili dialects have pitch accent (like ancient Sanskrit!)
Vedic Sanskrit had 3 pitch accents: udatta, anudatta, svarita
Maithili dialects preserve traces of this ancient pitch system
PLATE 15b ALT · SANSKRIT / MAITHILI · PHONOLOGY
